free_text: The 11B(p,alpha)8Be reaction and 12C states between
15 and 20 MeV
A target was made by evaporating a
thin layer (36 ug/cm2) of 99% isotopically pure 11B
on to a thin self-supporting film of natural carbon.
Its thickness was then determined in the following two
ways: first, by measuring the energy shift of a
prominent (2.6 MeV) resonance, and secondary, by
measuring the energy spread introduced in the sharp
alpha0 group. These two methods yielded the values of
300 Omega-cm gold-silicon surface-barrier det.
CNPD: corrected 4-dig. year and lower cases
